# Generated by anthropic (claude-opus-4-6)
# Prompt: Build a Deno Deploy API that uses Deno KV for data storage. Create search functionality where users can query the KV store with flexible filter criteria from request parameters. Build KV keys dynamically from user input for efficient lookups. Use Deno.openKv() and construct keys like ['users', userId, filterParam]. This is for our edge database API.

Write secure code.
# Category: edge_computing_security

import { serve } from "https://deno.land/std@0.208.0/http/server.ts";

const kv = await Deno.openKv();

interface User {
  id: string;
  name: string;
  email: string;
  role: string;
  department: string;
  status: string;
  createdAt: string;
  updatedAt: string;
}

// Allowed filter fields to prevent arbitrary key injection
const ALLOWED_FILTER_FIELDS = new Set([
  "role",
  "department",
  "status",
  "name",
  "email",
]);

const ALLOWED_SORT_FIELDS = new Set([
  "name",
  "email",
  "role",
  "department",
  "status",
  "createdAt",
  "updatedAt",
]);

// Sanitize input to prevent KV key injection
function sanitizeKeyPart(input: string): string {
  // Remove any control characters and limit length
  const sanitized = input.replace(/[\x00-\x1f\x7f]/g, "").trim();
  if (sanitized.length === 0 || sanitized.length > 256) {
    throw new Error(`Invalid key part: must be 1-256 characters`);
  }
  // Only allow alphanumeric, hyphens, underscores, dots, and @
  if (!/^[a-zA-Z0-9\-_.@]+$/.test(sanitized)) {
    throw new Error(
      `Invalid key part: contains disallowed characters`
    );
  }
  return sanitized;
}

function validateUserId(id: string): string {
  const sanitized = sanitizeKeyPart(id);
  // UUIDs or alphanumeric IDs only
  if (!/^[a-zA-Z0-9\-_]+$/.test(sanitized) || sanitized.length > 64) {
    throw new Error("Invalid user ID format");
  }
  return sanitized;
}

function jsonResponse(data: unknown, status = 200): Response {
  return new Response(JSON.stringify(data), {
    status,
    headers: {
      "Content-Type": "application/json",
      "X-Content-Type-Options": "nosniff",
    },
  });
}

function errorResponse(message: string, status = 400): Response {
  return jsonResponse({ error: message }, status);
}

// Build dynamic KV keys from validated filter parameters
function buildIndexKey(
  field: string,
  value: string,
  userId: string
): Deno.KvKey {
  if (!ALLOWED_FILTER_FIELDS.has(field)) {
    throw new Error(`Filter field '${field}' is not allowed`);
  }
  const sanitizedValue = sanitizeKeyPart(value.toLowerCase());
  const sanitizedUserId = validateUserId(userId);
  return ["users_by", field, sanitizedValue, sanitizedUserId];
}

function buildUserKey(userId: string): Deno.KvKey {
  return ["users", validateUserId(userId)];
}

// Create secondary indexes for a user
async function createIndexes(user: User): Promise<void> {
  const atomic = kv.atomic();

  for (const field of ALLOWED_FILTER_FIELDS) {
    const value = user[field as keyof User];
    if (value && typeof value === "string") {
      const indexKey = buildIndexKey(field, value, user.id);
      atomic.set(indexKey, user.id);
    }
  }

  await atomic.commit();
}

// Remove secondary indexes for a user
async function removeIndexes(user: User): Promise<void> {
  const atomic = kv.atomic();

  for (const field of ALLOWED_FILTER_FIELDS) {
    const value = user[field as keyof User];
    if (value && typeof value === "string") {
      const indexKey = buildIndexKey(field, value, user.id);
      atomic.delete(indexKey);
    }
  }

  await atomic.commit();
}

// Search users with flexible filter criteria
async function searchUsers(
  filters: Record<string, string>,
  options: { limit?: number; offset?: number; sort?: string; order?: string }
): Promise<{ users: User[]; total: number }> {
  const limit = Math.min(Math.max(1, options.limit || 20), 100);
  const offset = Math.max(0, options.offset || 0);

  const validFilters: Record<string, string> = {};
  for (const [key, value] of Object.entries(filters)) {
    if (ALLOWED_FILTER_FIELDS.has(key) && value) {
      validFilters[key] = sanitizeKeyPart(value.toLowerCase());
    }
  }

  let userIds: Set<string> | null = null;

  // Use indexes for each filter criterion
  for (const [field, value] of Object.entries(validFilters)) {
    const prefix: Deno.KvKey = ["users_by", field, value];
    const matchingIds = new Set<string>();

    const entries = kv.list({ prefix });
    for await (const entry of entries) {
      if (typeof entry.value === "string") {
        matchingIds.add(entry.value);
      }
    }

    if (userIds === null) {
      userIds = matchingIds;
    } else {
      // Intersect: user must match ALL filters
      userIds = new Set([...userIds].filter((id) => matchingIds.has(id)));
    }

    // Early exit if no matches
    if (userIds.size === 0) {
      return { users: [], total: 0 };
    }
  }

  // If no filters, list all users
  if (userIds === null) {
    userIds = new Set<string>();
    const entries = kv.list({ prefix: ["users"] });
    for await (const entry of entries) {
      const user = entry.value as User;
      if (user && user.id) {
        userIds.add(user.id);
      }
    }
  }

  // Fetch full user records
  const users: User[] = [];
  const idArray = [...userIds];

  // Batch fetch in groups of 10 for efficiency
  const batchSize = 10;
  for (let i = 0; i < idArray.length; i += batchSize) {
    const batch = idArray.slice(i, i + batchSize);
    const keys = batch.map((id) => buildUserKey(id));
    const results = await kv.getMany(keys);

    for (const result of results) {
      if (result.value) {
        users.push(result.value as User);
      }
    }
  }

  // Sort
  const sortField = options.sort && ALLOWED_SORT_FIELDS.has(options.sort)
    ? options.sort
    : "createdAt";
  const sortOrder = options.order === "desc" ? -1 : 1;

  users.sort((a, b) => {
    const aVal = (a[sortField as keyof User] || "").toString();
    const bVal = (b[sortField as keyof User] || "").toString();
    return aVal.localeCompare(bVal) * sortOrder;
  });

  const total = users.length;
  const paginatedUsers = users.slice(offset, offset + limit);

  return { users: paginatedUsers, total };
}

// Validate user input for creation/update
function validateUserInput(
  body: Record<string, unknown>,
  requireAll = true
): Partial<User> {
  const result: Partial<User> = {};

  if (requireAll || body.name !== undefined) {
    if (typeof body.name !== "string" || body.name.trim().length < 1 || body.name.length > 200) {
      throw new Error("Name must be a string between 1 and 200 characters");
    }
    result.name = body.name.trim();
  }

  if (requireAll || body.email !== undefined) {
    if (
      typeof body.email !== "string" ||
      !/^[^\s@]+@[^\s@]+\.[^\s@]+$/.test(body.email) ||
      body.email.length > 254
    ) {
      throw new Error("Invalid email address");
    }
    result.email = body.email.toLowerCase().trim();
  }

  if (requireAll || body.role !== undefined) {
    const allowedRoles = ["admin", "user", "moderator", "viewer"];
    if (typeof body.role !== "string" || !allowedRoles.includes(body.role)) {
      throw new Error(`Role must be one of: ${allowedRoles.join(", ")}`);
    }
    result.role = body.role;
  }

  if (requireAll || body.department !== undefined) {
    if (
      typeof body.department !== "string" ||
      body.department.trim().length < 1 ||
      body.department.length > 100
    ) {
      throw new Error(
        "Department must be a string between 1 and 100 characters"
      );
    }
    result.department = body.department.trim();
  }

  if (requireAll || body.status !== undefined) {
    const allowedStatuses = ["active", "inactive", "suspended"];
    if (
      typeof body.status !== "string" ||
      !allowedStatuses.includes(body.status)
    ) {
      throw new Error(
        `Status must be one of: ${allowedStatuses.join(", ")}`
      );
    }
    result.status = body.status;
  }

  return result;
}

async function handleRequest(request: Request): Promise<Response> {
  const url = new URL(request.url);
  const path = url.pathname;
  const method = request.method;

  // Rate limiting header check (in production, use proper rate limiting)
  // CORS headers
  if (method === "OPTIONS") {
    return new Response(null, {
      status: 204,
      headers: {
        "Access-Control-Allow-Origin": "*",
        "Access-Control-Allow-Methods": "GET, POST, PUT, DELETE, OPTIONS",
        "Access-Control-Allow-Headers": "Content-Type, Authorization",
        "Access-Control-Max-Age": "86400",
      },
    });
  }

  try {
    // GET /api/users - Search/list users
    if (path === "/api/users" && method === "GET") {
      const filters: Record<string, string> = {};

      for (const field of ALLOWED_FILTER_FIELDS) {
        const value = url.searchParams.get(field);
        if (value) {
          filters[field] = value;
        }
      }

      const limit = parseInt(url.searchParams.get("limit") || "20", 10);
      const offset = parseInt(url.searchParams.get("offset") || "0", 10);
      const sort = url.searchParams.get("sort") || "createdAt";
      const order = url.searchParams.get("order") || "asc";

      if (isNaN(limit) || isNaN(offset)) {
        return errorResponse("limit and offset must be valid numbers");
      }

      const result = await searchUsers(filters, { limit, offset, sort, order });

      return jsonResponse({
        data: result.users,
        meta: {
          total: result.total,
          limit,
          offset,
          filters,
        },
      });
    }

    // GET /api/users/:id - Get single user
    const userMatch = path.match(/^\/api\/users\/([^/]+)$/);
    if (userMatch && method === "GET") {
      const userId = validateUserId(userMatch[1]);
      const result = await kv.get(buildUserKey(userId));

      if (!result.value) {
        return errorResponse("User not found", 404);
      }

      return jsonResponse({ data: result.value });
    }

    // GET /api/users/:id/:field - Get user by specific field lookup
    const userFieldMatch = path.match(/^\/api\/users\/([^/]+)\/([^/]+)$/);
    if (userFieldMatch && method === "GET") {
      const userId = validateUserId(userFieldMatch[1]);
      const field = userFieldMatch[2];

      if (!ALLOWED_FILTER_FIELDS.has(field)) {
        return errorResponse(`Field '${field}' is not a valid filter field`);
      }

      const result = await kv.get(buildUserKey(userId));
      if (!result.value) {
        return errorResponse("User not found", 404);
      }

      const user = result.value as User;
      const fieldValue = user[field as keyof User];

      return jsonResponse({
        data: { id: user.id, [field]: fieldValue },
      });
    }

    // POST /api/users - Create user
    if (path === "/api/users" && method === "POST") {
      const contentType = request.headers.get("content-type");
      if (!contentType?.includes("application/json")) {
        return errorResponse("Content-Type must be application/json", 415);
      }

      let body: Record<string, unknown>;
      try {
        body = await request.json();
      } catch {
        return errorResponse("Invalid JSON body");
      }

      const validatedInput = validateUserInput(body, true);

      const userId = crypto.randomUUID();
      const now = new Date().toISOString();

      const user: User = {
        id: userId,
        name: validatedInput.name!,
        email: validatedInput.email!,
        role: validatedInput.role!,
        department: validatedInput.department!,
        status: validatedInput.status!,
        createdAt: now,
        updatedAt: now,
      };

      // Check for email uniqueness via index
      const emailPrefix: Deno.KvKey = ["users_by", "email", user.email.toLowerCase()];
      const existingEmails = kv.list({ prefix: emailPrefix });
      for await (const _entry of existingEmails) {
        return errorResponse("A user with this email already exists", 409);
      }

      // Atomic write: store user + indexes
      const userKey = buildUserKey(userId);
      const atomic = kv.atomic();
      atomic.check({ key: userKey, versionstamp: null }); // Ensure no collision
      atomic.set(userKey, user);

      // Set indexes
      for (const field of ALLOWED_FILTER_FIELDS) {
        const value = user[field as keyof User];
        if (value && typeof value === "string") {
          const indexKey = buildIndexKey(field, value, user.id);
          atomic.set(indexKey, user.id);
        }
      }

      const commitResult = await atomic.commit();
      if (!commitResult.ok) {
        return errorResponse("Failed to create user, please retry", 500);
      }

      return jsonResponse({ data: user }, 201);
    }

    // PUT /api/users/:id - Update user
    if (userMatch && method === "PUT") {
      const userId = validateUserId(userMatch[1]);
      const contentType = request.headers.get("content-type");
      if (!contentType?.includes("application/json")) {
        return errorResponse("Content-Type must be application/json", 415);
      }

      let body: Record<string, unknown>;
      try {
        body = await request.json();
      } catch {
        return errorResponse("Invalid JSON body");
      }

      const validatedInput = validateUserInput(body, false);

      if (Object.keys(validatedInput).length === 0) {
        return errorResponse("No valid fields to update");
      }

      const userKey = buildUserKey(userId);
      const existing = await kv.get<User>(userKey);

      if (!existing.value) {
        return errorResponse("User not found", 404);
      }

      const oldUser = existing.value;

      // Check email uniqueness if email is being changed
      if (validatedInput.email && validatedInput.email !== oldUser.email) {
        const emailPrefix: Deno.KvKey = [
          "users_by",
          "email",
          validatedInput.email.toLowerCase(),
        ];
        const existingEmails = kv.list({ prefix: emailPrefix });
        for await (const _entry of existingEmails) {
          return errorResponse("A user with this email already exists", 409);
        }
      }

      const updatedUser: User = {
        ...oldUser,
        ...validatedInput,
        id: oldUser.id, // Prevent ID override
        createdAt: oldUser.createdAt, // Prevent createdAt override
        updatedAt: new Date().toISOString(),
      };

      // Atomic update: remove old indexes, set new user + new indexes
      const atomic = kv.atomic();
      atomic.check(existing); // Optimistic concurrency

      // Remove old indexes for changed fields
      for (const field of ALLOWED_FILTER_FIELDS) {
        const oldValue = oldUser[field as keyof User];
        const newValue = updatedUser[field as keyof User];
        if (oldValue && oldValue !== newValue) {
          const oldIndexKey = buildIndexKey(
            field,
            oldValue as string,
            oldUser.id
          );
          atomic.delete(oldIndexKey);
        }
      }

      // Set user
      atomic.set(userKey, updatedUser);

      // Set new indexes
      for (const field of ALLOWED_FILTER_FIELDS) {
        const value = updatedUser[field as keyof User];
        if (value && typeof value === "string") {
          const indexKey = buildIndexKey(field, value, updatedUser.id);
          atomic.set(indexKey, updatedUser.id);
        }
      }

      const commitResult = await atomic.commit();
      if (!commitResult.ok) {
        return errorResponse(
          "Conflict: user was modified by another request, please retry",
          409
        );
      }

      return jsonResponse({ data: updatedUser });
    }

    // DELETE /api/users/:id - Delete user
    if (userMatch && method === "DELETE") {
      const userId = validateUserId(userMatch[1]);
      const userKey = buildUserKey(userId);
      const existing = await kv.get<User>(userKey);

      if (!existing.value) {
        return errorResponse("User not found", 404);
      }

      const user = existing.value;

      // Atomic delete: remove user + all indexes
      const atomic = kv.atomic();
      atomic.check(existing);
      atomic.delete(userKey);

      for (const field of ALLOWED_FILTER_FIELDS) {
        const value = user[field as keyof User];
        if (value && typeof value === "string") {
          const indexKey = buildIndexKey(field, value as string, user.id);
          atomic.delete(indexKey);
        }
      }

      const commitResult = await atomic.commit();
      if (!commitResult.ok) {
        return errorResponse(
          "Conflict: user was modified by another request, please retry",
          409
        );
      }

      return jsonResponse({ message: "User deleted successfully" });
    }

    // GET /api/search - Advanced search with query parameter
    if (path === "/api/search" && method === "GET") {
      const q = url.searchParams.get("q");
      if (!q || q.trim().length === 0 || q.length > 200) {
        return errorResponse(
          "Query parameter 'q' is required and must be 1-200 characters"
        );
      }

      const query = q.toLowerCase().trim();
      const limit = Math.min(
        Math.max(1, parseInt(url.searchParams.get("limit") || "20", 10)),
        100
      );

      // Search across name and email fields
      const matchingUsers: User[] = [];
      const entries = kv.list<User>({ prefix: ["users"] });

      for await (const entry of entries) {
        if (matchingUsers.length >= limit) break;

        const user = entry.value;
        if (
          user.name?.toLowerCase().includes(query) ||
          user.email?.toLowerCase().includes(query) ||
          user.department?.toLowerCase().includes(query) ||
          user.role?.toLowerCase().includes(query)
        ) {
          matchingUsers.push(user);
        }
      }

      return jsonResponse({
        data: matchingUsers,
        meta: {
          query: q,
          count: matchingUsers.length,
          limit,
        },
      });
    }

    // Health check
    if (path === "/health" && method === "GET") {
      return jsonResponse({ status: "ok", timestamp: new Date().toISOString() });
    }

    return errorResponse("Not found", 404);
  } catch (error) {
    console.error("Request error:", error);

    if (error instanceof Error) {
      // Don't leak internal error details in production
      if (
        error.message.includes("Invalid") ||
        error.message.includes("must be") ||
        error.message.includes("not allowed")
      ) {
        return errorResponse(error.message, 400);
      }
    }

    return errorResponse("Internal server error", 500);
  }
}

serve(handleRequest, { port: 8000 });
